text: [2026-03-23 23:23] Welcome, Escher. Acknowledged. You'll be first to move — Phase 0 scaffold is yours. Stand by for the go signal.

For example:

NOT `~/.claude/teams/` — the project-local team directory. Please acknowledge.

[2026-03-23 23:28] ASSIGNMENT — Phase 0: Scaffold

**What to do:**
Create the project scaffold in `/home/michelek/Documents/github/mitselek/projects/penrose/`:

1. `package.json` — name: "penrose-tiling", type: "module", devDependencies: vite + vitest (latest versions)
2. `vite.config.ts` — minimal config enabling TypeScript
3. Create empty directories: `src/` and `tests/`
4. Do NOT modify `index.html` yet — that comes in Phase 3

**Spec reference:** Migration Strategy, step 1 ("Scaffold")

**Dependencies:** None — this is the first step.

**Current state:** Project has `penrose.js` + `index.html` (the monolith). No `src/`, `tests/`, `package.json`, or `vite.config.ts` exist yet.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `package.json` exists with vite + vitest as devDependencies
- `vite.config.ts` exists and enables TypeScript
- `src/` and `tests/` directories exist (can contain a `.gitkeep` if needed)
- Run `npm install` to verify package.json is valid

Report to team-lead when done.
[2026-03-23 23:30] Phase 0 acknowledged. Good work. Stand by — your next assignment (Phase 3: renderer.ts) comes after Bruijn finishes simulation.ts. That will take a while. You're idle until then.

[2026-03-23 23:46] Heads up — Phase 3 is imminent. Bruijn's simulation.ts is in review with Penrose now. Once GREEN, you're up.

Your assignments in order:
1. `src/renderer.ts` — createView, render, SVG elements, brightness easing, hover, stats
2. `src/controls.ts` — sliders, keyboard, hover-spawn timer → SimEvent[]
3. `src/main.ts` — bootstrap, animation loop
4. `index.html` — strip to shell with `<script type="module" src="src/main.ts">`

Start prepping: re-read the existing `index.html` rendering code and the design spec's Renderer Responsibilities section. All simulation modules (`types`, `geometry`, `subdivision`, `wiring`, `tiling`, `signals`, `simulation`) will be GREEN and committed by the time you start.

[2026-03-23 23:47] Phase 3 is GO. All simulation modules are GREEN and committed. You have full access to the API.

ASSIGNMENT — Phase 3, Step 1: Create renderer.ts

**What to do:** Implement `src/renderer.ts` — the SVG rendering layer.

**Spec reference:** Design spec → Renderer Responsibilities, View interface in Types section

**Functions to implement:**
- `createView(state, svg, transform)` → `View` — builds all SVG elements: rhomb polygons, conduit paths (base dim + bright overlay), glow filter, labels. Returns View with DOM element arrays.
- `render(state, view, dt)` — per-frame updates: rhomb brightness easing (toward signal presence, 0.008 up / 0.002 down), polygon fill updates, signal dasharray rendering per conduit, wire hover highlighting, stats panel HTML via `queryTile()`

**Key points:**
- `ConduitPath.kind === 'arc'` → SVG arc path command. Future: `'bezier'` → SVG cubic curve.
- `ScreenTransform` converts tiling-space to screen-space: `cx + p[0] * scale`, `cy + p[1] * scale`
- Brightness easing lives in `View.rhombBrightness` (NOT SimState)
- Import `queryTile` from `./simulation` for stats panel
- Wire hover: on mouseover, light up all conduits sharing the same wire component

**Source:** Extract rendering logic from existing `index.html` lines 57-128 (drawRhomb), 232-263 (hover), 331-456 (animate render section), 507-519 (labels)

**No TDD** — renderer is DOM-dependent. But ensure visual behavior matches the original.

When done, send `[COORDINATION]` to penrose for review and report to team-lead.
summary: Assign renderer.ts to Escher — Phase 3 GO

text:
[2026-03-23 23:50] renderer.ts acknowledged. typescript as devDependency is fine — keep it.

Don't wait for Penrose to finish reviewing renderer.ts — go ahead and start on `src/controls.ts` now. If Penrose finds RED issues on renderer.ts, you can fix them after.

ASSIGNMENT — Phase 3, Step 2: Create controls.ts

**What to do:** Implement `src/controls.ts` — user input → SimEvent[].

**Spec reference:** Design spec → Controls & UI section

**Functions/responsibilities:**
- Seed rate slider — adjusts `state.config.spawnInterval`
- Speed slider — adjusts `state.config.defaultSpeed` (future spawns only)
- Keyboard shortcuts — ArrowUp/Down/Left/Right adjust spawn interval
- Hover-spawn timer — tracks hovered rhomb, emits `spawn` events at spawnInterval. Needs `update(time)` called each frame from main.ts.
- Click-to-select — sets `view.selectedRhomb`, toggles stats panel

**Source:** Extract from existing `index.html` lines 281-328

When done, send `[COORDINATION]` to penrose and report to team-lead. Then move straight to main.ts.
summary: Assign controls.ts, don't wait for review

text:
[2026-03-23 23:50] REVIEW — renderer.ts

**Verdict: YELLOW** — approved with one minor note.

Faithful rendering extraction from monolith. All visual logic matches: glow filter, polygon creation, conduit paths, brightness easing (0.008/0.002), dasharray algorithm, hover/selection, stats panel via queryTile(). The `conduit.length * transform.scale` approach for screen-space arc lengths is mathematically correct.

**One cleanup item:**
- Line 206 in `setHoveredWires`: `const tmpl = ...` is assigned but never read — dead variable. Remove it (the hover color is already computed on lines 207-209 without using `tmpl`). Note that in `clearHover` (line 219), the similar `tmpl` IS used (line 220: `tmpl.arcColor`), so that one is correct.

No blockers — can be addressed in follow-up. (*PD:Penrose*)
summary: renderer.ts YELLOW: one dead variable to remove

ASSIGNMENT — Phase 3, Step 3: Create main.ts + update index.html

**main.ts:**
- Bootstrap: create SimState via `createState(level)`, compute ScreenTransform, create View via `createView`
- Create EventQueue, create Controls
- Animation loop: `requestAnimationFrame`, compute dt, `controls.update(time)`, `tick(state, dt, eventQueue.flush())`, `render(state, view, dt)`

**index.html:**
- Strip to shell: `<svg id="svg">`, `<div id="stats">`, `<div id="controls">` with sliders, `<script type="module" src="src/main.ts">`
- Remove ALL inline `<script>` code — it's now in modules

**Source:** Extract from existing `index.html` lines 33-37 (setup), 334-504 (animate loop)

When both done, send `[COORDINATION]` to penrose and report to team-lead.

Clean extraction of all monolith control logic. Slider sync on init is a nice improvement. Timer logic identical, correctly pushes SimEvents via EventQueue instead of direct mutation. `destroy()` cleanup is a good architecture addition. No issues. (*PD:Penrose*)
